cmes.note: Very faint and narrow CME to the SE seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. No clear source for it has been found in GOES SUVI imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note:
Approximate analysis based on the fit of this very faint front in all three coronagraphs, 
SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. No source has been found in the GOES SUVI EUV imagery to confirm this analysis.

cmes.note: Very faint minor CME seen to the SE seen in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. No clear source has been found for this very faint minor CME in EUV imagery, however there is a very minor dimming at ~(S35E55) seen in GOES SUVI 195 imagery, tentatively confirming the analysis based on the fit of the front of the CME in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A coronagraphs.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the fit of this very faint, narrow front in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No clear source has been found for this very faint minor CME in EUV imagery, however there is a very minor dimming at ~(S35E55) seen in GOES SUVI 195 imagery, tentatively confirming the analysis based on the fit of the front of the CME in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A coronagraphs.

cmes.note: Large partial-halo CME visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an X1.4 flare from Active Region 14405 (S27E45) peaking at 2026-03-30T03:19Z. The source is associated with brightening, dimming, moving/opening field lines, and EUV wave as seen in GOES SUVI 195, 304, 284, 131, 094, and 171 starting around 2026-03-30T02:56Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Final analysis of the brighter bulk of this CME based on the location of the X-class flare and associated eruption, made with fully available coronagraph imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and a couple of GOES CCOR-1 images.
A very speculative analysis of the faint shock of this CME using mostly the SOHO LASCO imagery since there was no good fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2).
VERY PRELIMINARY MEASUREMENT based on first few frames in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is unknown but may be related to outflow/continuous eruptive activity from Active Region 14405 which was located near S27E38.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-03-30T17:23Z, as well as by GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a data gap which began at 2026-03-30T15:36Z. The source of this event is a small region of brightening and moving field line seen beginning at approx. 2026-03-30T16:30Z, located at approx. N01E58 as seen in GOES SUVI 131/195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-03-30T17:38Z, as well as by GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a data gap which began at 2026-03-30T15:36Z. The source of this event is likely continued field line movement seen around Active Region 14405 (S27E5) as seen in GOES SUVI 195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-03-30T20:30Z. This event is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a data gap which began at 2026-03-30T15:36Z, and is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-03-30T18:23Z. The source of this event is likely continued field line movement seen around Active Region 14405 (S27E5) as seen in GOES SUVI 195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the East by GOES CCOR-1 beginning at 2026-03-30T21:15Z. This event is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a data gap which began at 2026-03-30T15:36Z, and is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-03-30T18:23Z. The source of this CME is a small region of brightening and field line opening from Active Region 14408 (N09E55) seen beginning at approx. 2026-03-30T20:20Z in GOES SUVI 131/195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE by GOES CCOR-1 beginning at 2025-03-30T21:30Z. This event is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a data gap which began at 2026-03-30T15:36Z, and is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-03-30T18:23Z. The source of this CME is continued outflow and field line movement from Active Region 14405 (S27E31) as seen in GOES SUVI 195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1 in later frames.

cmes.note: This faint CME is visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2 following a lower C-class flare with associated filament eruption and moving/opening field lines starting around 2026-03-31T01:00Z as seen in GOES SUVI 304 and 284. This CME is not visible in GOES CCOR-1 due to an Earth-shine event, in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap from 2026-03-30T18:23Z to 2026-03-31T03:23Z, and in SOHO LASCO C3 due to faintness of event. There is likely deflection of this event due to a coronal hole located due south of the erupting region.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on potential source location and best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 imagery. The front of this CME is diffuse and may cause some uncertainty with parameters. This CME is not clearly seen in other coronagraphs due to various reasons (see main CME note).

cmes.note: Very faint CME visible to the S in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source appears to be a filament eruption along/beyond the southern limb starting around 2026-03-31T07:36Z in GOES SUVI 304 with associated opening/moving field lines as seen in GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Very faint CME visible to the N/NW in SOHO LASCO C2 and barely in SOHO LASCO C3. This CME is too faint to see in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source appears to be a flare and filament eruption from Active Region 14403 starting around 2026-03-31T10:25Z as seen in GOES SUVI 131, 304, and 284. Associated moving/opening field lines are also visible in GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on source and adjusted for best fit of visible features in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. Deflection is visible in the first few initial frames in SOHO LASCO C2 to the NW. This is likely due to the proximity of a coronal hole.

cmes.note: Faint CME to the NW observed only in SOHO LASCO C2, as the front dissipates after a few frames. The source is likely AR 14403, with some weak field line movement in SDO AIA 171/193 observed starting around 2026-03-31T23:16Z. Although this source and faint front are not significant or easy to match alone, it matches the signature of prior CME 2026-03-31T11:24Z also from AR 14403, corroborating this source.

cmeAnalyses.note: 3D SOHO LASCO C2 only analysis using W12 as the source longitude. CME front dissipates after 3 visible frames.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, due its faintness and ongoing GOES CCOR-1 Earth eclipse it may not present in the other coronagraphs. The source may be AR 14405 (S27E17) from an area slightly NW of the AR center (S21E07), observed as field line movement in SDO AIA 171/193 and warping of a dark area in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-04-01T01:43Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 with available imagery of the faint front.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2, possibly too faint or not yet seen in other available coronagraph imagery and/or blocked by the GOES CCOR-1 Earth eclipse. The source is an eruption from AR 14403 starting around 2026-04-01T02:03Z in SDO AIA 171/193/304 as brightening centered around N20W11, field line movement, and filament ejecta extending narrowly up to the NE from the eruption, appearing to largely dissipate or reabsorb to the disk. Also seen in STEREO A EUVI 195/304 as the same brightening, field line movement, and filament ejecta.

cmeAnalyses.note: 3D SOHO LASCO C2 only analysis, using the W11 source longitude, finding a further southern latitude than expected. With the filement ejecta poorly erupting and coronal hole proximity, deflection is likely, and the lack of a visible front in STEREO A COR2 means no triangulation possible.

cmes.note: Faint, fast CME observed to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, not in GOES CCOR-1 due to Earthshine. The source is unclear, but may be an eruption from AR 14405 with some field line movement in SDO AIA 171/193 and dimming in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-04-01T01:43Z, although that source better lines up with existing CME 2026-04-01T02:36Z. The CME may have deflected from the source location due to proximity to a coronal hole.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 before a downlink gap begins at 2026-04-01T05:48Z, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source may possibly be eruptive activity from AR 14405 (S27E16), as activity is seen from 2026-03-31T21:00Z to 2026-04-01T02:19Z. There is possible deflection due to coronal hole proximity.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulated analysis between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. Half width visibly <10 degrees, approximately 5 degrees or less.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1, not in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a real-time downlink gap. The potential source may be an eruption best seen in GOES SUVI 284 seen starting around 2026-04-01T03:07Z as dimming and moving field lines from AR 14405 (S27E15)

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement with additional available SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Blocked by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3.

Preliminary STEREO A COR2 plane-of-sky as SOHO LASCO C2/C3 are not available in real-time and triangulation fits with available GOES CCOR-1 were not yielding a good fit. STEREO A currently at W54.2 degrees, with a plane-of-sky at E36.

cmes.note: CME visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2. The likely source is a filament eruption centered at approximately S15W60 as seen in SDO AIA 193 imagery as dimming. Filament liftoff is best seen in GOES SUVI 304 starting around 2026-04-01T03:12Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the source location centered around longitude W60, but the filament eruption extends across at least 40 degrees, based on SUVI imagery.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE in STEREO A COR2. Also seen to the south in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and faintly to the south in GOES CCOR-1. The potential source is a filament liftoff best seen from the point of view of STEREO A EUV 304 starting at 2026-04-01T05:45Z. The eruption is also visible south of disk center in SDO AIA 304 imagery, around S40W15. There is possible deflection due to the northern extension of the southern coronal hole.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: Narrow CME visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2. Likely blocked by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3, and not clearly visible in GOES CCOR-1 or STEREO A COR2. The source is not seen in available EUV imagery, and is likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky measurement using SOHO LASCO C2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is faint but visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery. The source is likely an eruption from Active Region 14403 (N15W20) starting around 2026-04-01T13:30Z which appears to deflect northward in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the source longitude.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the east in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not observed by STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap in real-time starting at 2026-04-01T11:38Z. The source is likely the C5.3 flare from Active Region 14409 starting around 2026-04-01T13:38Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the source longitude.

cmes.note: Relatively slow filament eruption CME seen to the west in SOHO C2, SOHO C3, not as well in GOES CCOR-1, and not at all in STEREO A COR2A due to a data gap. The source is a filament eruption from near S20W55 near the eastern vicinity of Active Region 14399 (S16W62) seen best in SDO AIA 304 and GOES SUVI 304 which may have triggered a secondary eruption near or behind the western limb. The CME is relatively slow and contains a few moving fronts, the fastest of which is likely associated with the filament eruption itself.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement of the fast portion/leading edge of the CME which is most likely to be associated with the filament eruption seen in SDO AIA 304 imagery.

cmes.note: CME seen to the east in SOHO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 that does not have many EUV signatures off the eastern limb but appears to be from far-sided activity. May be associated, with lower confidence, with a minor eruption in Active Region 14409 (N02E26) which seems to have a more Earth-directed EUV signatures in GOES SUVI 284 and less of an easterly component; therefore, confidence seems to be higher that the eruption originates from behind the Earth-facing eastern limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Low confidence measurement of the CME using GOES and SOHO and the assumption that the CME is far-sided; Earth-sided fits were also able to be conducted for this CME with some poor skill.

cmes.note: Large partial halo CME seen in GOES CCOR-1 with brighter bulk mostly directed to the northwest with wider, fainter shock extending from the north to the southwest starting around 2026-04-01T23:45Z in association with a large-scale, strong filament eruption occurring near N28W25 around 2026-04-01T23:00Z with an associated long-duration C-class flare; the eruption is characterized by destabilizing filament channel starting as early as 22:30Z in SDO AIA 193 followed by a lift off of the filament channel best seen in SDO AIA 304 and GOES SUVI 304 with bright post eruptive arcades and a broad, large dimming siganture seen best in SDO AIA 193. The core prominence structure and the widely opening field lines are also seen very well in GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary shock front measurement using only a few early available frames of GOES CCOR-1.
Updated bulk measurement with more GOES CCOR-1 frames out to solar 13 solar radii but still without SOHO imagery due to no downlinked imagery at the time of measurement.

Preliminary bulk measurement using only a few early available frames of GOES CCOR-1 and knowledge of the source eruption. There is considerable uncertainty in velocity due to only a few frames being available at the time of measurement.

cmes.note: CME seen to the west in GOES CCOR-1. Not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2, and not visible in real-time in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a gap in the downlink. The potential source is an eruption south of AR 14401, approximately N05W70, as seen in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery starting at 2026-04-02T03:55Z. Moving field lines can also be seen near the west limb in GOES SUVI 284 and in SDO AIA 171 imagery in ISWA.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the potential source location using GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME visible to the NW in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. The potential source is an eruption starting around 2026-04-02T05:35Z as best seen in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery as dimming near AR 14401 (N25W75).

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME visible to the SE in GOES CCOR-1 imagery, the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and the S in STEREO A COR2. The CME is partially obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3. The source is a far-sided filament eruption which can be seen lifting off beyond the SE limb in GOES SUVI 304 imagery starting around 2026-04-02T05:42Z. The eruption can also be seen in Solar Orbiter EUI/FSI 304 low latency imagery at 2026-04-02T06:03Z, centered around S20E140.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the east in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures observed at the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely related to eruptive activity from Active Region 14401/14402 (N20W70) starting around 2026-04-02T11:35Z as seen in STEREO A EUVI 195 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A imagery.

cmes.note: First of two part CME associated with an M3.5-class solar flare and subsequent filament eruption taking place between 2026-04-02T16:00Z and 21:00Z, the CME is narrow and seen to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2 and not yet seen in STEREO A COR2A due to a data gap. There is a faint EUV wave seen best in SDO AIA 171 showing that the eruption may have been deflected slightly to the north.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary leading edge measurement out to 13 solar radii using SOHO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Second of a two-part CME associated with an M3.5-class solar flare in Active Region 14404 (N12W18) and subsequent filament eruption taking place between 2026-04-02T16:00Z and 21:00Z, the CME is narrow and seen to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2, more northerly as compared with the earlier 2026-04-02T18:48Z CME but closely following behind it, and not yet seen in STEREO A COR2A due to a data gap. There is a filament eruption clearly seen after 19:30Z best seen in GOES SUVI 304 which is the likely source and suggests the eruption may have been deflected slightly to the north relative to the source.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement using SOHO C2, C3 and GOES CCOR-1 which suggests the filament eruption CME is wider than previously thought--its width increases as it propagates outward into the field of view, especially in SOHO C2/C3.
Early preliminary leading edge measurement using SOHO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 using only just three available frames.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the NE in STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3, not yet visible in GOES CCOR-1 but may be obscured by its pylon regardless. The source may be a dimming region centered around N18E08, NE of AR 14409, observed starting to dim around 2026-04-04T03:00Z and speeding up around 2026-04-04T06:28Z in SDO AIA 171/193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis with additional imagery from LASCO and STEREO A COR2, based on the fit of the fuzzy faint front in two coronagraphs. There seems to be some eastward deflection from the dimming considered to be the source of this CME.
Preliminary triangulation with early available frames in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 as only visibility. CME appears deflected from the observed soure.
